question: A worker is annotated as shown and the properties also declare `camunda.client.worker.override.charge-card.fetch-variables=["customerId"]`. Which variables does the broker fetch on activation?

codeBlocks.stem:
@JobWorker(type = "charge-card", fetchVariables = {"orderId", "amount"})
public void chargeCard(JobClient client, ActivatedJob job) { ... }

options.text: Only `customerId` — the override replaces the list from the annotation.
`orderId`, `amount`, and `customerId` — the override extends the annotation's list.
All process variables, because any override implicitly forces fetching everything.
Only `orderId` and `amount` — the annotation always wins over the override.

a.text: The docs are explicit: when `fetch-variables` is set in an override, it replaces the list of variables to fetch. So only `customerId` is fetched on activation.

b.text: Extension behaviour applies only when `fetch-variables` is set in `defaults`. For an override under a specific job type, the list is replaced, not merged.

c.text: Fetching all variables is controlled by `force-fetch-all-variables`, which is not set here. Setting `fetch-variables` in an override does not force fetching all.

d.text: The override wins for that job type: it explicitly replaces the annotation's `fetchVariables` list, so the annotation's values are not used.

explanation: Under `camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type>.fetch-variables`, the docs state that when set in an override, the list replaces the variables to fetch. To extend rather than replace, the property must be set under `camunda.client.worker.defaults` instead.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
